channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Diablo 4 Cow Level Seemingly Found, but There Could Be More
item.content:encoded:
Cow levels have been a tradition in the Diablo series since the halcyon days of Diablo 2, and now, it seems that players have finally found and completed Diablo 4's version of the long-running meme. Is that the end of things, though?

As reported by Eurogamer, YouTuber LoatheBurger appears to have become the first Diablo 4 player to have discovered the game's secret cow level, outlining the steps required to do so in a video a few days ago.

Unlocking the cow level is, as you've probably already surmised, a pretty involved process. It requires gathering items from across Diablo 4's world, and it also needs you to have the latest Lord of Hatred expansion, which might explain why nobody has managed to make this discovery before.

Although the cow level has now been discovered and completed, that might not be the full stop it sounds like. As Eurogamer points out, LoatheBurger subsequently found that the Cow King boss fought during the secret cow level drops an item called "Prime Rib".

The item description for the Prime Rib item is as follows:

A royal cut of beef, with perfect marbling, a majestic texture, and a regal flavor. Such a prime cut would be a worthy offering to anyone, even to a god.

Now, this could just be a fun little bit of flavor text (if you'll pardon the pun), but the "god" mentioned in the item description could also hint towards a larger purpose for the Prime Rib item. Inevitably, LoatheBurger will be the first one to discover that purpose if it exists.

If you want to catch up with Diablo 4's cow level, you can check the game out right now on PC, PlayStation, and Xbox, and the new Lord of Hatred expansion is also available now across all of those platforms. Be sure to check out our very own Austin's review of the base game as well!

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
